questions.text: What was the statue of the Happy Prince covered with?
questions.explanation: The statue was gilded all over with thin leaves of fine gold.

questions.text: What were the eyes of the Happy Prince made of?
questions.explanation: For eyes, he had two bright sapphires, which had been brought from India.

questions.text: What was on the hilt of the Prince's sword?
questions.explanation: A large red ruby glowed on his sword-hilt.

questions.text: Which bird decided to rest at the feet of the Happy Prince?
questions.explanation: A little Swallow, who was flying to Egypt, decided to stay for a night.

questions.text: Why was the Happy Prince crying?
options: He could see the misery and ugliness of the city
questions.explanation: Though he was called 'Happy', from his high pedestal he could see the suffering of the poor.

questions.text: To whom did the Prince give the first ruby?
questions.explanation: He sent the ruby to a seamstress whose little boy was ill with fever.

questions.text: Where were the Swallow's friends waiting for him?
questions.explanation: His friends had already flown to Egypt and were waiting near the Nile.

questions.text: What did the Swallow do for the seamstress's boy?
questions.explanation: The Swallow fanned the boy's forehead with his wings to help him feel cool.

questions.text: To whom was the first sapphire given?
options: A young playwright in a garret
questions.explanation: The Prince gave it to a poor young man trying to finish a play but was too cold and hungry to write.

questions.text: Who received the second sapphire?
questions.explanation: He gave his other eye to a little girl whose matches had fallen in the gutter.

questions.text: What happened to the Prince after giving away both sapphires?
questions.explanation: As the sapphires were his eyes, he was now blind.

questions.text: Why did the Swallow decide to stay with the Prince forever?
options: He was too tired to fly
questions.explanation: He decided to stay because of the Prince's kindness and because he was now blind.

questions.text: What did the Prince ask the Swallow to do with the gold leaves?
options: Pick them off and give to the poor
questions.explanation: The Prince wanted to help all the hungry children in the city with his gold coating.

questions.text: As the winter came, what happened to the little Swallow?
questions.explanation: The severe cold was too much for the little bird, and he died at the Prince's feet.

questions.text: What happened to the Prince's heart when the Swallow died?
options: It leaden heart snapped into two
questions.explanation: A curious cracking sound rang inside the statue, as if something had broken.

questions.text: Why did the Mayor want to pull down the statue?
options: He wanted to build a new one
The statue looked 'shabby' and no longer beautiful
questions.explanation: Since the gold and jewels were gone, the statue looked like a beggar to the Mayor.

questions.text: What part of the statue would NOT melt in the furnace?
questions.explanation: The broken lead heart would not melt and was thrown on a dust-heap.

questions.text: What two precious things did the Angel bring to God?
options: The leaden heart and the dead bird
questions.explanation: God declared these as the most precious things in the city.

questions.text: The Happy Prince lived in the palace of ______ when he was alive.
questions.explanation: Sans-Souci means 'without sorrow', and he never knew tears there.

questions.text: The little Swallow fell in love with a ______ earlier in the story.
questions.explanation: He had stayed behind because he was in love with a beautiful Reed.

questions.text: What did the Prince say about 'more marvelous than anything'?
options: The suffering of men and women
questions.explanation: He believed that human misery was the greatest mystery and most important concern.

questions.text: How did the Swallow try to keep himself warm?
questions.explanation: He flapped his wings to generate heat but eventually succumbed to the cold.

questions.text: What was the author's name for this story?
questions.explanation: Oscar Wilde is the famous author of 'The Happy Prince'.

questions.text: The statue stood on a ______.
questions.explanation: The statue was placed high above the city on a tall column.

questions.text: What did the swallow think about the prince's heart being made of lead?
options: He didn't say it aloud as he was polite
questions.explanation: The swallow was too polite to make personal remarks about the prince not being 'solid gold'.

questions.text: The playwright was eating ______ to keep warm.
questions.explanation: He was hungry and there was no fire in the grate.

questions.text: The matchgirl's matches were spoiled because they fell in the ______.
questions.explanation: Her matches fell in the gutter and were all spoiled.

questions.text: God promised the Swallow that he would sing in the garden of ______.
questions.explanation: God said the bird would sing forever in his garden of Paradise.

questions.text: The Happy Prince and the Swallow represent:
questions.explanation: The story is a parable about true beauty lying in compassion and helping others.

questions.text: The Mayor decided that 'Dead birds must not ______'.
questions.explanation: He wanted a law that birds should not be allowed to die near statues.

questions.text: The Prince's sapphires were ______ years old.
questions.explanation: The story mentions they were brought from India a thousand years ago.

questions.text: What was the Swallow's final action before dying?
options: He kissed the Prince on the lips
questions.explanation: He kissed the Happy Prince and then fell down dead at his feet.

questions.text: The seamstress was embroidering ______ on a satin gown.
questions.explanation: She was embroidering passion-flowers for the Queen's maid-of-honour.

questions.text: What did the Town Councilor say about the Prince?
options: He is as beautiful as a weathercock
questions.explanation: He wanted to seem practical, so he added 'but not quite so useful'.

questions.text: The little Swallow said 'I am going to the House of ______'.
questions.explanation: He realized he was dying and called death the brother of sleep.

questions.text: The Prince saw two hungry boys ______.
options: Lying in each other's arms under a bridge
questions.explanation: He saw them trying to keep each other warm in the cold night.

questions.text: The Mayor wanted to melt the statue and make a statue of ______.
questions.explanation: The Mayor and each of the Town Councilors wanted a statue of themselves.

questions.text: The Swallow's heart was full of ______ for the Prince.
questions.explanation: He delayed his journey and sacrificed his life because he loved the Prince.

questions.text: What color was the sapphire?
questions.explanation: Sapphires are typically bright blue gems.

questions.text: The story ends on a ______ note.
questions.explanation: The recognition of their souls in heaven gives the story a positive, divine ending.
